item.title: I owe my life to imgur. An update on the 18 year old who imgur treated like family.
item.description:
I don't know If anyone will see this post but honestly I just want to say thank you to everyone who helped me and was the mom/parent I never had.
(My brothers apartment with his husky Syrus) I was physically and emotionally abused by my mother for the 18 years and 2 months I lived with her. I was told I was stupid, I'd never do anything, that I should never have kids and that no one loves me. I was over medicated by my mother so then I would be come as docile and dependent as she could make me. I was locked in my room for a year and a half with an alarm on the door so I could never leave, I could only leave to go to the bathroom and to go to school, she gave us a PBJ sandwich for every meal we were home for. Last September (02/21 I turned 18 4 weeks earlier) my mother was kicking me and stepping on my hands trying to hurt me. In the process of me trying to get a way she lost balance and broke her wrist. She then called the police and had me arrested and I went to jail. I got out 12ish hours later. My dad who I hadn't had a relationship for with 5ish years and had lived with for 6+ years was coming down from Idaho to pick me up. My older brother and my step mom came with him. My dad got me a hotel that night and the 3 of them drove all night from Idaho to pick me up. I was still in shock from the whole situation and couldn't stop crying. My brother and my dad picked me up from the hotel and they gave me a hug that makes made me want to not let go. We then went to pick up my step mom from her relatives house. We then drove 7 hours to boise Idaho, dropped off my brother at his apartment (I hadn't seen him for 2 years) and my step mom, dad and me went to their house. We got me signed up for school and I started going. There's stuff I should mention just incase. My step mom did not like me mainly became she was very controlling of my dad and I was kinda an outside voice talking to him. I wanted to be as nice as I could to them so I sucked up to my step mom and tried my hardest to be likeable. I cleaned their kitchen, did dinner, made jokes, etc. A few weeks pass and my dad tells me that my step mom doesn't like me and that she doesn't like that I've been going out of my way for her to like me. I was shocked and told him I didn't mean to offend her. I apologized to her the next day. 2 weeks go by and my step mom makes my dad choose me or her. My dad chose her. My brother who lives with his wife, 14 yo kid and husky in a 2 bed apartment picked me and my stuff up from my dad's house and I moved in with them. I lived on their floor for 2 weeks until I had a previous imgur post.
(The boise river that was less than a mile from my apartment) Thanks to imgur I was able to take over a lease at a shared apartment by a college. I had 1 roommate who actually gave me my first shot of whiskey (I am very light weight and almost threw it up). I lived there for 4(honestly lost track I was in a very bad mental state) and worked and rode my bike 14 miles to school. When I signed the lease I was not told I wouldn't be able to renew it, and was told I'd have to move in July. I eventually had to drop out of school because of bills I had to pay and my degraded mental health. July grew closer and closer and I was not able to find an apartment for less than $1300 (not including utilities) I was working as a chef at a retirement home at the time. July arrived and I had to move into my brother's house for 2 weeks. I had a court date In late July(or August, busy year for me) and had to go to Utah. The judge told me that i will have to pay for all the money my mom lost on wages and all of her bills/money spent due to her injury. He told me it was like $1500. When in utah I did some thinking about what I can afford and what I want in my life.
(My dad gets free standby because he works for united, the flight from Idaho to Denver) I was able to find a cheap apartment ($950 including utilities) in Ogden utah. I talked to my dad about it and we decided that because the rent was so cheap it would be good financially for me. I was able to get setup with a work from home job at Stroable as customer service representative. They have amazing benefits and provide me with wifi money and all needed equipment. In the following week I was able to get hired onto the job and get a start date. The next day after a lot of planning my dad drove me to the boise airport where I would fly on standby to Denver then to slc and have my uncle pick me up and drive me to the apartment. I looked at the recently renovated apartment and it was amazing for the price they were asking. I signed the papers later in the evening. My dad was amazing and cosigned with me. I sadly got debt because of surprise expenses the apartment required.
(The apartment I live in now) I moved into the apartment the next day with only a few pairs of clothes and a blanket. A week went by and my dad drove down with some extra house hold items for me (silverware, air mattress, cups, etc.) And he brought lots of ramen and we bought some cheap chicken. I moved to Utah about 5 months ago (I think I got that right) and I've been at the same job the entire time. I have imgur to thank for helping me when I needed help and been the support I never had. I probably forgot to say something or mention something, it has been a long year and stuff does get forgotten. Also I was in a relationship for 8 months with my girlfriend (I was gonna merry her but decided that I need to focus on me) so I haven't been alone all this time. I also did get a support animal that is making a HUGE impact on me and I literally love him to death. In short I owe imgur a lot and I am so thankful for every single one of you who has reached out to me, interacted with me, or been there when I needed it.

item.title: Ukraine Update Feb 22/23: Putin invades, Ukraine mobilizes, Germany acts.
item.description:
President Zelensky remains defiant in the face of Russian aggression. This is not pure hubris as the fully mobilized Ukrainian Armed Forces stand at over 600,000 strong, larger then the armed forces of France, Germany, and the UK combined. This post is a long one and it is not uplifting. Lots of diplomatic movement as Russia justifies its aggression and the West attempts to solidify a unified response. As before no Ukrainian troop moments or deployments will be posted. Russian deployments will be posted. Edit: An hour after this was posted Putin announced a start to military operations. War has begun. Слава Україні та її Воїнам!
Ukraine enters a state of emergency. The new measures include restrictions of spreading (mis)information as well as a ban on reservists and conscripts relocating.
Vice Speaker of the People's Council of the LPR Dmitry Khoroshilov calls on Ukraine to voluntarily withdraw troops from all of the Luhansk region, otherwise they will take action. This is the lie Putin is using to start a war. First there are the false flag attack to generate a crisis. Then he recognized puppet regimes which claim massive amounts of land still controlled by Ukraine. Finally those puppets ask Putin for help taking Ukrainian land.
Putin's decree recognizing the breakaway regions is just a copy/paste of the one used after the 2008 invasion of Georgia. Russian actions are a farce. They would be a comedy if not for the likely end result being tens of thousands dead.
The order sending Russian forces into Ukraine.
German Chancellor Scholz orders a halt to the Nord Stream 2 pipeline project. Actual constructive action from Germany!
Graffiti in the Moscow metro: "No to war!"
UR-77 Meterorit mine-clearing vehicle of the Russian army in Donetsk.
Russian Rosgvardia (occupation troops) prisoner transports need the Ukrainian border.
1/3
2/3
3/3 Kenya strongly denounces Russian imperialism in Ukraine.
This tiny crowd was all that could be gathered for the cameras in Donetsk to celebrate Russia declaring them independent states.
Pro-Ukraine rally outside the Russian embassy in Berlin.
Moscow metro: "No to war"
After this farce of an exchange Russia did quickly admit to recognizing the borders of the entire region, of which 2/3 are controlled by Ukraine. Still, it is important to record and recognize how Russia has been acting.
Conscription off the streets continues in Donetsk.
False Flag. Russia/DNR claim a Ukrainian Army attack including the vehicle was repulsed. The vehicle is a BTR-70M, which is not used by the Ukrainian Army.
Russian armor headed to the front loaded down with supplies and ammunition. This is a solid sign of imminent (hours/days) war.
Roughly 130,000 Ukrainian men and women have volunteered for service in the Territorial Defense Battalions, light infantry militia who will be tasked with slowing any Russian attack.
Japan, Taiwan, and Singapore all join in economic action against Russian aggression. American leadership in globally coordinating action has been exceptional. The differences from the mediocre actions of 2014 and the unhelpful acts of the previous US administration are stark.
EU begins sanctions.
Ukrainian airports in Kharkiv, Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia are closed until morning and Ukraine is blocking runways in case of possible attack.
Ukraine starts mobilization. The government is hesitant to move to full mobilization as the economic effects would be catastrophic. Western economic aid is urgently needed to allow Ukraine to fully focus on the physical defense of its territory.
Russian Leer-3 Electronic Warfare system is being used to send text messages to Ukrainian forces.
Canada begins sanctions.
Australia begins sanctions and gives visa priority to Ukrainians but says it will not provide Ukraine with any arms.
Switzerland decides money is more important then principles.
New NOTAM tells aviators to avoid the Ukraine/Russia border.
Russian yacht "Ragnar", was boarded by the Norwegian Coast Guard, Police and Customs today. The owner is one of Putin's circle, former KGB officer Vladimir Strzhalkovsky. Early reports are the boat was used for cutting fiber optic cables
Russian TV shows a map of Ukraine with only the small section in yellow being labeled as Ukraine.
Russian forces enter occupied Luhansk.
The now largely empty Pogonovo assembly area, which housed the 1st Tank Army for months. The Tank Army is now deployed at the Ukrainian border.
More Canadian arms are flown into Ukraine.
Bernie Sanders condemns Russian aggression, calls for action.
Air traffic over Europe. Easy to find the economically isolated Ukraine.
While speaking this early on refugees may be a bad idea as it could undermine Ukrainian morale and is certainly no substitute for sending the arms needed to Ukrainians can continue to live in Ukraine this topic must be addressed. Estimates are at least 4,000,000 refuges in the case of invasion. The West must welcome them.
The UK begins sanctions.
Belgium, a nation with a massive arms industry, decides not to assist Ukraine.
American President Joe Biden announces sanctions.
Sweden refuses to call sending an army into another sovereign nation an "invasion".
Putin demands Ukraine surrender.
This crisis must be a wake up call for Europe.
We are entering the final act. Russia troops are entering their final forward staging areas and the can not remain there long. They soon must move into Ukraine and to war or back to Russia and to peace. Ukraine has begun a mobilization that will cripple its economy if continued too long. One must back down or there will be war on a scale not seen since WWII. Russia has rejected every diplomatic effort and is continuing its false flag attacks even after recognizing the rebel regions. Diplomacy only works if both sides want peace and I do not see that attitude in the Kremlin right now. The only hope I see remaining is that Ukraine and the West have enough strength, with both economic sanctions and physical arms, to make clear to Putin he will take massive unacceptable losses if he attacks. To be clear I do not think NATO will, or even should, go to war. What should be done is arms should be provided so Ukraine can defend itself and money provided so Ukraine does not have to worry about economic collapse. Reject misinformation. Stay informed. Stay safe. Разом переможемо! Слава Україні та її Воїнам!
item.dc:creator: RedE100

item.title: Dump of things my gfs and I send to each other
item.description:
Long long grapes
Senator Armstrong
No cats in the house at the moment, so have a pizza that is bound to upset some people but I will enjoy this glorified cheese/sauce breadbowl next time I find myself near GR. It was great last time I had it :p Edit: since I saw some clamoring about the pizza, tis a joint in Grand Rapids called Big Bob's Pizza. Great place a coworker introduced me to, would recommend giving it a shot if you're ever in town.

item.dc:creator: PiningForTheFeeeords

item.title: I panicked like I have never panicked before
item.description: So I'm a general contractor who mostly remodels bathrooms, but this was a first. Yesterday, on the final day of a bathroom remodel, I decided to be the first person to use the new toilet (the clients were at work). As usual, I did my business while lurking on Imgur, and I flushed the toilet a few times over the course of my poop. When I was finished, I looked over towards the tub and saw brown water with little lettuce-looking chunks coming out of the bathtub drain and filling the damn thing up. I panicked, and the first thing I could think to do was to flush the toilet again (maybe there was a tp clog that I could dislodge). But now the toilet appeared to be clogged, and it started filling to the brim with brown water. Fuck. I ran around the client's house looking for a plunger, but to no avail. I came back into the bathroom to see AN ACTUAL PIECE OF SHIT FLOATING AROUND IN THE SHOWER. Fuck Fuck Fuck. I remembered that that the waste lines were accessible in the garage and I sprinted downstairs, and that's when I saw it: there was still a rubber plug in the waste line from when the plumbers performed a water leakage test. Still panicking, I ripped the plug out of the waste clean-out and listened in relief as my poop gurgled down the 4 inch pipe. Then the fucking pipe "BURPED" and spit some of the heinous liquid onto my pants. But I wasn't even that mad because I knew that the bathroom wasn't full of my shit anymore. I spent the next hour cleaning the place up, went home, and was too embarrassed to tell anyone. So I'm telling you.
item.dc:creator: MacAndMemes

item.dc:creator: MisterKratos

item.title: Ukraine Update Feb 24: War.
item.description:
Putin declares war. With these words he condemns thousands to die. I fully expected this to happen but it still hurts. I will continue to post updates about what is now the Russo-Ukrainian War. I will not post Ukrainian troop movements or positions. I will verify to the maximum extent possible and give context. Factual information and context are my priories.
Ukrainian President Zelensky speaks to the nation. He says he's declared martial law and urges Ukrainians to stay home. “We are working. The army is working," he says. "Don't panic. We are strong. We are ready for everything. We will defeat everyone. Because we are Ukraine.”
Russian (former) opposition leader Alexei Navalny, currently on trial held while in prison(!) speaks out against the war. Alexei Navalny was poisoned by the FSB on the orders of Putin before being arrested. We know it was the FSB because he called up the agent who poisoned him posing as his superior and got him to confess live on camera. “I’m against this war […] it will lead to a huge number of victims, destroyed lives, and the future impoverishment of Russian citizens.”
Russian armor crosses from Belarus into Ukraine.
Pre-war polling shows that the Belarusian population did not want this war.
Russia claimed it is acting to protect civilians. What it is doing is firing large numbers of unguided or poorly guided weapons into dense urban areas.
Russian infantry under heavy fire from Ukrainian forces, reportedly on the outskirts of Schastia.
Ukraine begins Total Defense.
Map of the fighting, as best as it can be determined at this time.
Moldova borders Ukraine, is not NATO, and has a section of its nation occupied by Russia. This move is not unexpected.
The Russian financial sector has already taken massive losses. The Moex index is down 45% in early trading. Gazprom (Russian state owned energy) is down 48%.
A decade of growth gone in a moment, and for what? Pride and nostalgia for an empire three decades dead?
Lithuania is a NATO member which shares a border with Belarus, which is part of a "Union State" with Russia. Putin has claimed that Lithuania, like Ukraine, is rightfully Russian.
Russia has been targeting bridges in an attempt to slow Ukrainian movement.
The Chief of Germany Army posts on LinkedIn: "The Bundeswehr, and the Army that I have the privilege to lead, is more or less stripped bare. The options that we can offer politicians to support the alliance are extremely limited."
Civilians flee from Kyiv.
Russian anti-war protesters are being arrested by police.
The Ukrainian Forces are not standing idle, here launching rocket barrages against Russian forces.
An extremely Australian reporter films a Kh-55 cruise missile over Donetsk.
A Ukrainian Su-27 fighter has landed in Romana.
Statement from the Syrian Civil Defense on the conflict.
Russian Army forces enter from occupied Crimea.
Estonia is a NATO member which shares a border with Russia. Putin has claimed that Estonia, like Ukraine, is rightfully Russian. Estonia calls for NATO membership to be offered to Ukraine.
The President of Taiwan issues a statement condemning the invasion.
Sweden hardens its stance and condemns the Russian invasion. "Sweden condemns in the strongest terms Russia’s ongoing invasion of Ukraine. Russia’s acts are also an attack on the European security order. It will be met by a united and robust response in solidarity with Ukraine. Russia alone is responsible for human suffering."
Ukraine fully commits to war.
One of the final lighter pre-war acts that did not make it into the previous update.
Russian Soldiers of the 91701 Yampil Motorized Rifle Regiment captured by Ukraine.
A Ukrainian child walks past the remains of a Russian Kh-31 missile.
Even now Ukrainian morale is high.
Reposting this from a previous update. The US intelligence agencies have been correct about almost everything so far. They are probably correct about this as well.
It has been mere hours since my last update. How quickly the world can change. I still had the software I use to edit video down to imgur 60 sec limit open from my last update when I started on this one. I started these updates as a method of release, to shout into the void of the internet that something was happening. It was also to record the crisis and claim in real time so Putin could not rewrite history, or rather to making calling it out easier. Each update takes 3+ hours of research, gathering video and photos, and writing. The reception here shows that the truth still matters. Thank you. War has come to Europe. The End of History has ended. Ukraine is fighting hard but needs support. The democratic West must rise to the occasion. The free peoples of the world have prevailed against authoritarian conquers before and can do it once again. A word on known incorrect information. Much of it is not spread maliciously like the pre-war Russian false flags but is simply misunderstandings. A few are known to me. First there is the metadata showing Putin recorded his declaration of war days ago. This is actually from his history rant as shown by the 55min run time. His 28min declaration of war has metadata for the 24th. Air drops from exercises are also being shared as part of the current war. No large scale drops have happened so far. There are plenty of real Russian lies and crimes out there to show the world, so please check your sources to ensure we speak the truth. Recognize misinformation. Stay informed. Stay safe. Разом переможемо! Слава Україні та її Воїнам! For those who want to know how this tragedy came to happen please view my previous updates: Feb 22/23: https://imgur.com/gallery/Zcl7LES Feb 20/21: https://imgur.com/gallery/LvmiAM9 Feb 19: https://imgur.com/gallery/M1t8ia1 Feb 18: https://imgur.com/gallery/TBwhc69

item.dc:creator: kingbudo101

item.title: How history repeats itself...
item.description: I'm from Croatia, in the picture here is a water tower that was bombed in our war for independence in the 90s. That broken tower is one of the images of our war, a symbol of resistance and cruelty. I've been following the news with Ukraine, and hearing the things Putin says, the narrative that's being pushed there, it's all too familiar. I'm not even going to go into details, it's just eerily similar. Something that saddens me is when I go to our public forums, the same people that claim to have fought in our war are right now cheering for Putin. For days now, I've read those people claim Ukraine is not actually a country, or that Crimea belongs with Russia and this and that, and making fun of anyone trying to criticize Putin. I was young back then, but I remember the sound of sirens, I remember going to bunkers under buildings, picking up bits of shrapnel in the kitchen, missing out on school because it wasn't safe. I'm lucky it didn't last long. I'm sad it's happening again in Ukraine, I'm sad so many people have already decided that they don't care, I'm even more sad so many have decided to cheer on Putin and will grab on any straw just to derail the topic. Stay strong Ukraine!
item.dc:creator: AdamAstrum

item.title: a ha

item.dc:creator: Pookie55

item.title: Shit sucks, man
item.description: FP edit: thank y’all. Thank you for the kind words, for your advice, your messages, and for sharing your own experiences. I’m gonna try to answer everyone. This has really helped me navigate some of my feelings here because I didn’t even know where to start. It also helped mitigate some of my feelings of blame that it surprised me even popped up. I had no idea that miscarriage was this common. I truly love the Imgur community. We can’t really afford therapy and you guys have given me a jumping board on how to discuss this topic with my mom and my husband. Downvoters- I understand. This isn’t a fun topic, and certainly not the release, the escape, a lot of us use Imgur for. It can also be triggering for someone that’s been through it and has no desire to relive it. I apologize. I’m putting a tag on it for anyone that has this topic blocked. Not posting for sympathy, just needed to vent. Please. PLEASE. If someone is losing or has lost their child, here already or not, don’t tell them it’s ‘God’s plan’ or ‘they’re waiting for you in heaven’ or ‘they’re in a better place now’- we wanted them HERE. I’m only 6 weeks, but we were so excited we already have names picked out. My first doctor visit was supposed to be Monday. It ended up being yesterday when I woke up to a lot of bloody, watery discharge. They drew blood to check my hormone levels and they’ll do it again tomorrow. I already know what the results will be. Did you know the technical term for ‘likely miscarriage’ is ‘threatening abortion’? It was on my after-visit summary and I had to look it up. The bleeding has gotten heavier with clots and cramping now. My mom said to ‘not lose faith’ because many women bleed while pregnant. Every story I’ve read where the bleeding started in early pregnancy with a fluid release first then progressed to bleeding and clots ended in miscarriage. It’s your body initially releasing the fluids meant for the baby, to prepare to eject it, similar to your water breaking when it’s time to deliver. I’m ok. I’m having my moments of crying but I also have a son to take care of. I just wanted to re-iterate, and I know she means well, but I don’t need spiritual excuses for why we won’t have a newborn at the end of October. My little bumblebee is just in my heart now, and I’m making peace with that in the earthly realm. If you read this, thank you for listening.

item.title: A Reminder of Why They Still Support Him

item.title: Corporate America Sucks. So I took it for a ride...
item.description: TL;DR Job refused to consider cost of living adjustments, completely quit efforts to work and it took them almost 11 months to fire me. Story Time: I worked for one of the biggest telecom companies in the US. Started in the office, transitioned to Work From Home due to Covid. After half a decade and then some of asking for fair cost of living adjustments I was told no every single time. Was told we were already competitive so nope, not gonna happen. The job I had previously had done a COLA for its employees so I knew their refusal to consider it was bullshit. If they don't value me the right way, I shouldn't be here. So after several years of that, I resolved to quit. But I was going to make them do it. In early 2021, I killed the motor. I stopped trying and watched my once pristine metrics slowly tank. I sat through coaching after coaching with my sup, him trying methods to make me work harder, me smiling inside knowing that we are wasting our time because my time immersed in this culture has stolen my motivation. And I made it known, too. We had a regular eNPS survey that they say is anonymous, but it isn't. I let them have it in the past year on every single one. Our big manager had the gall to address my comments about COLA's and told us to stop asking. This is when I elevated my efforts. Since our pay was not effected by the quality of our work, just our hours, my path was clear; Escalate until it blows up! >:-D I invented system problems. Since I had different internet than work provided, I knew they had no in-house way to check whether I was actually in an outage so my home internet developed many frustrating problems during business hours (Which was little more than an unplugged modem cable). I waited days for techs that were never scheduled. I said my headset would not work when it was working fine. I kept feeling like someone with half a brain would see it and figure out what I was doing, but I was 7 months into not trying and still being paid in full for it. I wondered how far I could push it. I kept expecting a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) to be issued but eventually, my boss just decided that my magic was gone and he left me alone to put up mediocre numbers, while playing on my gaming PC for the entire shift, not caring about the work at all. Finally after about 8 months, they started to ask about the headset and it not working. They had finally noticed that I didn't care anymore, so surely my termination was imminent. Internet issues only cropped up when we were exceptionally busy. The holidays were approaching and my boss told me to order a new headset. Support tickets were opened to troubleshoot, but it was a weird gremlin that no one could pin down. Imagine that... It's not like there were pay raises and more opportunity available for hard work, so why try? Finally my boss questioned my metrics and said there was something going on. Deep Dives by others in the QC Dept finally revealed my actions of not caring. What I expected to get away with for a month or so, went on for nearly a year. It was 10.5 months after I killed my efforts before they terminated me. It seems A.I. systems that grade you for metrics aren't smart enough to detect actual thinking. What's funny is that he never said why. It was a very short Zoom call where I was informed of being terminated. I cashed out my stock portfolio I had been building with the company and am going to take the next year off before getting back into the work force again. Thanks Imgur, for allowing me a place to vent my story. Never working for Corp America again. It's only the CEO and Board's friend, certainly not its employees'. I still can't believe it took them as long as it did to catch it. Cheers, you wonderful bastards. Disclaimer: I did this knowing I was sinking the ship. Please don't do the same unless you are ready for the job to end, too.
item.dc:creator: DisgruntledTechWorker

item.title: A dump for sad nerds, part 1
item.description:
Twilight also had a great soundtrack: Muse, Radiohead, Sea Wolf, Death Cab For Cutie, Bon Iver, etc.
Dog tax: Amaterasu wants to play fetch with you. Do you accept?

item.dc:creator: SugarTeet

item.title: Not all women need a glass slipper to make dreams true
item.description: It took a marathon of Mike Rowe's "Dirty Jobs," me hating my career, and the news coverage of Hurricane Maria wiping Puerto Rico out to have me change careers in my 30s. I researched electrical tradework, and read that only 2% of electrical workers in the U.S. are women, so much to my mother's chagrin, I stopped doing what I was doing and went back to school. Today, I got my card. And the statistic moved upwards internationally, probably by a small margin of such a wee percent. Took 4.5 years to get here. Tell the good smart women in your life to do whatever it takes to move a statistic! Goal set: 2017 Goal achieved: 2022
item.dc:creator: Iwantsomepie
